component.name: OpenVPN3 GUI
component.summary: System tray indicator for OpenVPN3 VPN tunnels

description.p: A system tray application for managing OpenVPN3 VPN tunnels on Linux. Written in Rust with GTK4 for better performance and lower memory usage compared to the Python version.
Features:

ul.li: System tray icon with dynamic status (idle, connecting, active, paused, error)
VPN configuration management (import, remove)
Session management (connect, disconnect, pause, resume, restart, reconnect)
Desktop notifications for status changes (deduplicated, grouped per connection)
Secure credential storage via Secret Service (oo7)
Launch on login via XDG autostart (toggle in Preferences)
Auto-connect on startup (most-recent or specific profile)
Reconnect prompt on unexpected session drop
Username/password, challenge/OTP, and browser-redirect authentication
Tabbed session log viewer with live log streaming, per-tab search and log-level filter, filter-aware copy, and persistent window size

Tabbed Preferences dialog (General / Security / Routing) for startup, notifications, intervals, timeout, stall detection, kill-switch, split-tunneling bypass CIDR editor with per-entry enable/disable, and credentials
Configurable connection timeout with notification
Connection stall detection — flags an idle tunnel in tray menu and icon

Kill-switch — block all non-VPN traffic via nftables when a tunnel drops (requires the privileged helper package); always-visible state row (lock/unlock) in tray menu; lock indicator in session label when active; notifications on rule apply (persistent) and removal; helper-missing notification and Preferences hint; quit confirmation warns before removing rules

Split tunneling — exempt specific networks from the VPN tunnel so they route over the local connection (requires the privileged helper package); configure bypass CIDRs in the Preferences Routing tab with per-entry enable/disable checkboxes; always-visible state row in tray menu; notifications on route apply and failure; symmetric IPv4/IPv6 routing with MSS clamping
First-run help notification when the OpenVPN3 backend is not installed or reachable
Automatic recovery when the OpenVPN3 service restarts
Multiple VPN configuration support

description.p: Correctness release — five pre-existing bugs, several security-relevant:

ul.li: Kill-switch: disconnecting one session no longer strips firewall protection from other still-connected tunnels; a wrong-password auth-retry no longer drops the firewall mid-swap. (If rebind to a surviving tunnel can't anchor a live rule, the rules are torn down rather than left stale.)
Kill-switch / bypass: the physical interface's rp_filter is now restored correctly after a network change mid-VPN (e.g. WiFi → Ethernet) instead of being left loose.
Stats: Duration and Since now show real values for tunnels that were already up when the GUI launched, instead of "—".
Stall auto-reconnect now respects its cooldown across a session reconnect, instead of looping against a persistently dead server every stall interval.
An unexpected drop of a session that connected before the GUI saw it created now triggers reconnect/notification instead of being silently swallowed.

description.p: Internal: split credential_handler under the 800-line gate; consolidated duplicated bypass apply/teardown sequences; refactored the stats refresh helper. No behavior change beyond the fixes above.

Internal-only release closing the three-sprint complexity-reduction arc (S41–S43). No user-visible behaviour change; every high-cognitive-complexity hotspot in the tray app and killswitch helper has been decomposed into small, unit-tested pure functions behind thin async glue. Tagged as a minor version bump to mark the arc as a milestone rather than for any feature.

ul.li: Decomposed the status-change signal loop, statistics poller, credential dialog, preferences save path, tray-action dispatch, service-restart watcher, and notification builders — each split into pure, tested helpers (71 new tests) plus thin impure wiring.
Split the largest modules (stats_poller, status_handler) into concern-focused submodules under the file-size health gate.
The privileged killswitch helper's rule-apply path was refactored to extract its watcher teardown; all D-Bus input-validation guards were preserved unchanged.
Code review across the full arc confirmed the refactor is behaviour-preserving; pre-existing multi-session and reconnect-cooldown edge cases surfaced by the review are tracked for a future fix.

description.p: Internal cleanup sprint with one latency win: startup D-Bus work is parallelized and the config-name fallback is unified to a single source. No user-visible behaviour change beyond faster tray-population and consistent window titles.

ul.li: Startup now runs the config scan, the session scan, and the kill-switch helper version probe concurrently (try_join), cutting startup-to-tray from the sum of the three round-trip chains to their max.
Per-session cold-start reads (status, config name, config path) now run concurrently (join3) so N sessions cost N round-trips instead of 3N.
The config-name fallback is centralized ("VPN Connection") so a session dropped from the tray shows the same label in the tray, notifications, Logs, and Statistics windows.
The auth-retry decision is extracted into a unit-tested pure function; the two status-driven disconnect paths collapse into one helper.
Micro-cleanups: reconnect delay threaded as a parameter, a trivial firewall re-apply guard inlined, and status-change dispatch returns a typed reason instead of a re-derived boolean.

description.p: Internal complexity-reduction sprint — the four highest cognitive-complexity hotspots (the status-change handler, D-Bus initialization, session signal handlers, and the stats poller) are decomposed into unit-tested pure helpers and thin orchestration wrappers. No user-visible behaviour change.
Internal quality sprint — no user-visible behaviour change.

ul.li: Hardened the log viewer search filter: the lowercased-search contract is now enforced by a dedicated type, making a future caller that forgets to lowercase the search term a compile error rather than a silent case-mismatch bug.
Removed three stale dead-code suppression markers whose comments had been overtaken by call sites that landed in an earlier sprint.
Added unit tests for the bypass-route apply outcome mapping (success / partial / system-failure → tray state), extracted from the impure tray-and-notification wrapper.

description.p: Internal cleanup — no user-visible behaviour change.

ul.li: Removed a dead table-presence check from the kill-switch bypass-set drift detection, simplifying the drift-diff path (helper).
Reduced redundant string allocations in the log viewer search filter (lowercases the search term once per rebuild instead of once per log line).
Documentation sync between the GUI and helper CIDR family-splitting helpers.

description.p: Split-tunneling reliability: a new drift monitor verifies the firewall bypass sets still match your configured CIDRs while a tunnel is up, plus hardening fixes found in review.

ul.li: Split-tunneling drift detection — while a tunnel is connected, the app now periodically verifies the firewall bypass sets still hold your configured bypass CIDRs. If an external firewall manager, manual nft edit, or partial teardown removes or widens a CIDR, the tray shows a Split tunnel: N CIDR(s) missing (or unexpected CIDR(s)) warning with a persistent notification; the warning clears and a one-shot "Split Tunneling Restored" notice fires once the sets match again. (Requires the privileged helper package, v0.3.14+.)

Host-sized bypass entries (single IPv4 or IPv6 hosts) no longer register as a permanent false drift — the comparison now treats a host's bare firewall representation and its canonical /32|/128 form as the same entry.
Drift notifications no longer re-fire every poll cycle while drift persists; they re-notify only when the missing or unexpected CIDR count actually changes.
Internal: the status-change dispatcher's pure transition logic was extracted for unit testing, and the log viewer's search/level filter gained unit tests.

description.p: Credential reliability fixes closing the 0.3.11–0.3.12 arc — bug fixes only, no new features.

ul.li: Saved-credential operations now unlock the keyring before every read and write, not just some of them. On a locked keyring, "Remember" save, per-field delete, per-config Forget, global Clear-all, and the --clear-secret-storage startup flag previously no-op'ed silently (save appeared to succeed but stored nothing; deletes reported nothing removed). All now act, or report a clear failure.

The wrong-password auto-reconnect counter is reset when you manually Disconnect or Remove a configuration, so reconnecting with a corrected password gets the full 3 attempts again instead of inheriting the abandoned session's failures within the 5-minute window.

Locked-keyring errors now show a legible "Keyring is locked" message on the Clear-all and Forget actions (matching the existing save path), including when the unlock prompt is dismissed, instead of raw error text.

description.p: Credential isolation and per-config management — finishing the saved-credential reliability work begun in 0.3.11 (no new features beyond credential handling).

ul.li: Each VPN configuration now has its own per-config Forget Credentials action in the tray menu (Connect → Forget Credentials → Remove), removing that connection's saved username/password without touching the configuration itself or any other connection. Previously the only way to forget saved credentials was the global "Clear all saved credentials".

The 3-attempt wrong-password auto-reconnect counter is now isolated per configuration (keyed on its unique object path) instead of by display name, so wrong-password attempts on one same-named configuration no longer burn the retry budget of a sibling sharing that name.

description.p: Credential reliability and import feedback — hardening across saved-credential handling and config import (no new features).

ul.li: Saved credentials now pre-fill the login dialog even when the keyring (Secret Service) collection is locked: the keyring is unlocked before the dialog opens, and keyring errors are surfaced instead of silently blanking fields. A failed "remember credentials" save now shows a notification instead of no-oping silently.

Saved credentials are now keyed by the VPN configuration's unique object path rather than its display name. Because OpenVPN 3 allows two configurations to share a name, keying by name could cross-wipe a same-named sibling's stored identity on removal; keying by path isolates each connection. Existing saved credentials migrate to the new key automatically on first use (the legacy entry is left intact if migration fails).

Removing a VPN configuration now also wipes its stored username/password/OTP from the keyring, so rotating or deleting a connection no longer leaves orphaned credentials that could re-populate a same-named re-import with the wrong identity.

Importing a configuration now validates it: a malformed file is rejected with the parser's error and removed from the profile list, instead of being silently accepted. (Requires OpenVPN 3 daemon v22+; older daemons skip validation.)
Import and removal now always show an in-app result dialog, regardless of the desktop-notification setting, so a successful import is no longer silent when notifications are off.
The 3-attempt auth-retry counter (wrong-password auto-reconnect cap) is now covered by unit tests.

description.p: Sprint 33: kill-switch fail-closed completion and one GUI correctness fix (no new features).

ul.li: The helper now restores the physical interface's rp_filter to its pre-apply value when it shuts down (SIGTERM/SIGINT). Previously a shutdown with bypass routing active left rp_filter stuck at loose mode until reboot.
Re-applying bypass routes (on reconnect or bypass-list change) no longer overwrites the stored original rp_filter value, so the eventual teardown restores the true original instead of a no-op.

An errored VPN session is no longer masked by the stall/idle warning icon: stall detection is now gated on the Connected status, closing a race where the stats poller re-armed the idle flag after a status transition had cleared it.

description.p: Kill-switch fail-closed fixes: rp_filter is now restored if bypass-route apply fails mid-chain; bypass routing is torn down when the GUI crashes (not just the firewall); re-applying rules no longer opens a no-enforcement window (the teardown and rebuild run in one atomic nft transaction).

Helper hardening: nft, ip and conntrack are invoked via absolute paths so a root service never trusts ambient PATH; conntrack CIDR-flush semantics verified and documented; the routing-teardown loop cap is now tied to the single bypass-CIDR limit constant.
Sprint 31: GUI correctness and robustness hardening (no new features)

ul.li: Idle/stall warning now respects the configured threshold — a single zero-delta poll no longer surfaces the warning icon prematurely.

Idle detection now accumulates correctly across polls — the stall timer is no longer reset every cycle, so the warning icon, idle label, and stall-driven auto-reconnect actually fire once a tunnel goes quiet past the threshold.
Error state always takes priority over the idle warning — an errored session shows the error icon even if it was previously idle.
Poison-tolerant locks — a panic while holding a global lock no longer bricks later disconnect and auth bookkeeping.
System-bus reconnection recovery — a transient system-bus outage at startup no longer requires a GUI restart for the kill-switch helper to reconnect.
Config-import dialog now routes through the shared singleton path; generic notifications route through the dedup map to prevent stacking.
Internal: gsettings and log-viewer modules split for headroom under the size threshold.

description.p: Sprint 30: auto-reconnect, log export, statistics dialog, and split-tunnel PoC validation

ul.li: Auto-reconnect after unexpected disconnect — when enabled, the app waits a configurable delay (5–300s, default 30s) then reconnects automatically. Falls through to the existing reconnect notification if the attempt fails.

Auto-reconnect on stall — a connected session that stops moving bytes past the stall threshold is now also treated as an unexpected drop when auto-reconnect is on. Cooldown window prevents reconnect loops against a permanently dead server.

Log export to file — new Export button in the log viewer toolbar writes the active tab's filtered entries (respecting search and level filter) as a plain-text file for bug reports or offline analysis.

Connection statistics dialog — new per-session Statistics menu item opens a live-refreshing window showing config name, status, connected-since timestamp, duration, tunnel interface, and byte counters. Auto-closes when the session disconnects.

Split-tunnel PoC script — conntrack/MTU probes now target a known-reachable control host so they no longer SKIP on networks that filter the bypass destination; new test-with-killswitch command validates bypass traffic through the kill-switch; test-resume automated via D-Bus.

description.p: Sprint 29: dialog lifecycle hygiene, cold-start auth, and About styling

ul.li: Dialog singleton registry — Preferences, View Logs, About, and other windows now reuse a single instance instead of stacking duplicates on repeated tray clicks
Modal funnel — when a confirmation dialog is open, any tray action raises the modal instead of opening a new window, preventing dead-lock behind an invisible grab
Preferences is now non-modal so tray notifications remain visible while configuring settings
Cold-start authentication — sessions already waiting for credentials when the GUI starts now prompt the user automatically instead of showing "Authentication required" with no dialog
Unified auth dialog — all authentication flows (credentials, challenge/OTP) now route through a single 3-field dialog (username, password, authentication code) with keyring pre-fill
About dialog — replaced GtkAboutDialog with libadwaita AboutWindow, fixing the blue headerbar artifact on GNOME

description.p: Sprint 28: service-lifecycle robustness and per-CIDR split-tunneling status

ul.li: Connect: retry NewTunnel with exponential backoff (500 ms / 1 s / 2 s) so the first connect after a fresh login no longer fails when the openvpn3-sessions service has not yet been D-Bus activated

Watcher: recover gracefully when the openvpn3-sessions service is killed — clear stale tray sessions, drop kill-switch and bypass routes at the helper, and notify the user instead of leaving zombie entries that silently reject Disconnect/Resume/Restart

Split tunneling: per-CIDR success/failure reporting — tray label distinguishes "N networks" (all applied), "N active, M failed" (partial), and "Apply failed" (system-wide); a persistent notification lists up to five failing CIDRs so the user knows exactly which subnets did not route outside the tunnel

Helper: ApplyBypassRoutes now returns per-entry granularity instead of failing the whole batch on the first bad CIDR; MIN_HELPER_VERSION raised to 0.3.5 to match the wire-incompatible signature change

description.p: Sprint 27: split-tunneling DNS-leak documentation, build-step assertions, resume-auth diagnostics

ul.li: Docs: surface DNS-leak caveat for split tunneling in README and the Routing preferences tab — bypass routes destination IPs only, query names still hit the system resolver
Docs: full code-path + failure-mode analysis of the DNS resolver path appended to docs/split-tunneling.md
Diagnostics: info-level logs for resume-after-pause re-auth dispatch (session_ops, status_handler) to disambiguate the long-pause re-credential flow
Build: Makefile dist targets (deb, deb-helper, rpm, rpm-helper) now assert artifact presence with ls -la after each cargo invocation — mirrors the release.yml post-conditions

description.p: Sprint 26: per-entry bypass toggle, AUR helper packaging, close-out

ul.li: Split tunneling: per-entry enable/disable checkboxes in the Routing tab — suspend a bypass CIDR without losing the row
Packaging: AUR PKGBUILD for the privileged kill-switch helper
Packaging: removed unreferenced killswitch SVG icons from DEB/RPM assets
Docs: kill-switch and split-tunneling design docs refreshed to match current code
Failure modes: silent-failure tail closed as won't fix with recorded rationale

description.p: Launch on login, notification improvements, release hardening

ul.li: Launch on login — XDG autostart toggle in Preferences General tab
Split tunneling: per-entry enable/disable checkboxes in the Routing tab — suspend a bypass CIDR without losing the row
Notifications: surface silent failures on kill-switch re-apply and menu actions
Release workflow: per-step artifact assertions catch silent build failures early
bump-version: awk-based prepend preserves all prior release entries
Packaging: AUR PKGBUILD for the privileged kill-switch helper

description.p: Patch release: CI packaging fix

ul.li: Release workflow: pre-create RPM output directory so cargo-generate-rpm produces correctly-named .rpm files (fixes v0.3.0 release shipping without RPMs)

description.p: Split-tunneling bypass, kill-switch hardening, and production readiness

ul.li: Split-tunneling: exempt specific networks from VPN via bypass CIDR editor (Preferences Routing tab)
Kill-switch: bypass tray indicator, lock icon in session label, persistent notifications, quit confirmation
Logs viewer: per-tab search and log-level filter, filter-aware copy, persistent window size
GSettings schema migration smoke test for safe upgrades
Connect-time failure-mode audit — silent errors now surface as notifications
RPM packaging fixes (asset paths, directory entries)
Version management: single source of truth from Cargo.toml, make bump-version target
